### Basic usage of ResponsiveContainer for a responsive chart Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/ResponsiveContainer.mdx This JSX snippet demonstrates how to wrap a Recharts chart component, such as `LineChart`, within a `ResponsiveContainer` to make it adapt to its parent's dimensions. It sets the width to fill 100% of the parent and a fixed height of 400px, allowing the child chart to automatically receive the calculated dimensions after client-side rendering. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### FunnelChart with ResponsiveContainer Parent Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/FunnelChart.mdx FunnelChart should be wrapped within a ResponsiveContainer component to enable responsive behavior and automatic sizing based on parent container dimensions. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Implement Responsive Charts with Recharts ResponsiveContainer in React Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt This React component illustrates the use of Recharts' ResponsiveContainer to create charts that automatically adapt to their parent container's size. It shows examples of percentage-based sizing, aspect ratio constraints, and how to use the debounce and onResize props for performance optimization and callback functionality. The ResponsiveContainer wraps LineChart instances, ensuring fluid and dynamic chart layouts. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { ResponsiveContainer, LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Page A', uv: 4000 }, { name: 'Page B', uv: 3000 }, { name: 'Page C', uv: 2000 }, { name: 'Page D', uv: 2780 }, { name: 'Page E', uv: 1890 }, ]; function ResponsiveExample() { return (
{/* Percentage-based sizing */} {/* Aspect ratio constraint */} {/* With resize callback and debounce */} console.log(`Chart resized to ${width}x${height}`)} >
); } export default ResponsiveExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Brush Component - Interactive Data Filtering and Zooming Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt Implements an interactive brush selector to filter and zoom through large datasets in a line chart. The Brush component displays a preview of data with adjustable range handles (startIndex and endIndex). It accepts an onChange callback to track range modifications and works with ResponsiveContainer for responsive layouts. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { LineChart, Line, Brush, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const generateData = () => { const data = []; for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) { data.push({ name: `Point ${i}`, value: Math.random() * 1000 + 100, }); } return data; }; const data = generateData(); function BrushExample() { return ( {/* Brush for data filtering */} console.log('Brush range:', range)} > ); } export default BrushExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### ResponsiveContainer with initialDimension for Server-Side Rendering (SSR) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/ResponsiveContainer.mdx This JSX example illustrates using `ResponsiveContainer` with the `initialDimension` prop to provide fallback dimensions for server-side rendering (SSR) environments. This ensures the chart renders with specified dimensions (e.g., 520x400) before client-side hydration, preventing content layout shifts while waiting for browser API calculations to determine actual dimensions. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Recharts LineChart with Default Accessibility (JSX) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/Accessibility.mdx This JSX code demonstrates how to render a basic Recharts LineChart within a ResponsiveContainer. In Recharts 3.0, keyboard navigation and screen reader support are enabled by default for all charts, meaning this chart will inherently support accessibility features without additional accessibilityLayer props. It utilizes Line, XAxis, YAxis, and Tooltip components to visualize data. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Line Chart with Multiple Data Series in Recharts Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt Build a multi-line chart component displaying sales, expenses, and profit trends over months. The LineChart uses ResponsiveContainer for automatic sizing, CartesianGrid for background grid, and multiple Line components with different stroke colors and styles. Includes interactive tooltips, legends, and custom dot styling. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Jan', sales: 4000, expenses: 2400, profit: 1600 }, { name: 'Feb', sales: 3000, expenses: 1398, profit: 1602 }, { name: 'Mar', sales: 2000, expenses: 9800, profit: -7800 }, { name: 'Apr', sales: 2780, expenses: 3908, profit: -1128 }, { name: 'May', sales: 1890, expenses: 4800, profit: -2910 }, { name: 'Jun', sales: 2390, expenses: 3800, profit: -1410 }, { name: 'Jul', sales: 3490, expenses: 4300, profit: -810 } ]; function LineChartExample() { return ( ); } export default LineChartExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### PieChart Component Hierarchy Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/PieChart.mdx Documents the valid parent and child component relationships for PieChart in Recharts. PieChart must be wrapped in ResponsiveContainer and supports multiple child components for customization. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Set Logarithmic Scale and Custom Domain for Recharts YAxis Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks This Recharts YAxis configuration applies a logarithmic scale using `scale="log"` to better visualize data with a wide range of values, like planetary masses. A custom domain `domain={[1e22, 3e30]}` is also specified, which is often required for the logarithmic scale to function correctly. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Customize X-axis ticks in Recharts LineChart with a functional component (TypeScript/JSX) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/GettingStarted.mdx This snippet demonstrates how to provide a custom rendering function for X-axis ticks, allowing for advanced customization of labels. It defines `CustomizedAxisTick` as a React functional component that rotates and repositions text, offering fine-grained control over tick appearance for better readability. This approach enables highly tailored chart aesthetics. ```tsx import React, { PureComponent } from 'react'; import { LineChart, Line, CartesianGrid, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip } from 'recharts'; const CustomizedAxisTick = (...args) => { const { x, y, stroke, payload } = args[0]; return ( {payload.value} ); }; const renderLineChart = ( } /> ); ``` ```tsx export const CustomizedAxisTick = (...args) => { const { x, y, stroke, payload } = args[0]; return ( {payload.value} ); }; ``` ```tsx } /> ``` -------------------------------- ### Adjust margin and stroke for Recharts LineChart components (TypeScript/JSX) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/GettingStarted.mdx This snippet demonstrates how to customize the appearance and layout of a Recharts line chart by adjusting component properties. It increases the chart's margin to accommodate labels and sets a dashed stroke style for the `CartesianGrid` for improved visual clarity. This allows for fine-tuning the chart's presentation. ```tsx import { LineChart, Line, CartesianGrid, XAxis, YAxis } from 'recharts'; const renderLineChart = ( ); ``` ```tsx ```
